questions: Which areas do you want to discuss for Phase 12 (Claude Code Remote Session)?

answers.Which areas do you want to discuss for P: Session purpose & scope, Session lifecycle, Session context, Dashboard integration

questionItems.question: Which areas do you want to discuss for Phase 12 (Claude Code Remote Session)?

options.label: Session purpose & scope
options.description: What can users do in the remote session? Just ask questions? Give instructions? Restart autopilot? What's the boundary between the session and the dashboard?

options.label: Session lifecycle
options.description: When does the session start/stop? Always running or on-demand? What happens if the session dies? Auto-reconnect?

options.label: Dashboard integration
options.description: Where does the session URL appear? Embedded iframe vs external link? How prominent? Alongside or replacing existing UI?

options.label: Session context
options.description: Does the session know about the current project/phase? Pre-loaded with context? Or a blank Claude Code session?

questions: What's the primary reason a user would open the remote session?

questionItems.question: What's the primary reason a user would open the remote session?

options.label: Ask questions about the build
options.description: "Why did you do X?" "What's the status of Y?" — conversational Q&A about what autopilot is doing

options.label: Give instructions to Claude
options.description: "Fix this bug", "Change the approach" — actively directing Claude Code to do work

options.label: Both equally
options.description: Session serves as both a Q&A window and a way to give Claude hands-on instructions

questions: Should the remote session be able to restart or control the autopilot process?

answers.Should the remote session be able to res: don't worry about claude code capability that is not your concern you should only be worried about creating the remote session and providing the URL through the dashboard.

questionItems.question: Should the remote session be able to restart or control the autopilot process?

options.label: Yes, restart autopilot
options.description: User can say "restart autopilot" or "stop autopilot" in the session and it actually controls the process

options.label: No, session is independent
options.description: The session is a separate Claude Code instance — autopilot control stays in the dashboard/CLI only

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on whether to wire up process control

questions: Should the remote session be created using `claude code --remote` CLI command, or the Claude Agent SDK programmatically?

answers.Should the remote session be created usi:
look at this https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control#new-session

Its actually 

claude remote-control

questionItems.question: Should the remote session be created using `claude code --remote` CLI command, or the Claude Agent SDK programmatically?

options.label: CLI command (claude --remote)
options.description: Spawn `claude --remote` as a child process, capture the URL from stdout

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on the best approach to create the remote session

questions: So the approach is: autopilot spawns `claude remote-control` as a child process, captures the session URL from stdout, and stores it in state. Is there anything else you envision beyond that for session creation?

questionItems.question: So the approach is: autopilot spawns `claude remote-control` as a child process, captures the session URL from stdout, and stores it in state. Is there anything else you envision beyond that for session creation?

options.label: That's it
options.description: Spawn the process, capture URL, store it, show it in dashboard. Done.

options.label: Also pass project dir
options.description: Make sure the remote-control session is started in the same project directory as the autopilot

questions: More questions about session purpose & scope, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about session purpose & scope, or move to next area?

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Session Lifecycle

options.label: More questions
options.description: I have more to say about purpose & scope

questions: When should the remote-control session start?

questionItems.question: When should the remote-control session start?

options.label: When autopilot starts
options.description: Automatically spawn `claude remote-control` alongside the autopilot process every time

options.label: On-demand via dashboard
options.description: User clicks a button in the dashboard to start the remote session

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on the best trigger

questions: If the remote-control process dies unexpectedly, what should happen?

questionItems.question: If the remote-control process dies unexpectedly, what should happen?

options.label: Auto-restart silently
options.description: Detect the crash, respawn `claude remote-control`, capture new URL, update dashboard

options.label: Notify and offer restart
options.description: Show an error in dashboard with a 'Restart session' button

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on recovery strategy

questions: When autopilot finishes (build complete), should the remote session stay alive or shut down?

questionItems.question: When autopilot finishes (build complete), should the remote session stay alive or shut down?

options.label: Stay alive
options.description: Keep the session running so users can still ask questions about the completed build

options.label: Shut down with autopilot
options.description: Clean up the remote session when autopilot exits

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: Should the user be able to opt out of the remote session (like --no-tunnel for dev-tunnels)?

questionItems.question: Should the user be able to opt out of the remote session (like --no-tunnel for dev-tunnels)?

options.label: Yes, --no-remote flag
options.description: Add a CLI flag to skip creating the remote session

options.label: No, always create it
options.description: Remote session is always spawned, no opt-out needed

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: More questions about session lifecycle, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about session lifecycle, or move to next area?

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Session Context

options.label: More questions
options.description: I have more to say about lifecycle

questions: `claude remote-control` is run in the project directory, so it has filesystem access. Should the session be pre-loaded with any specific context (like a system prompt or CLAUDE.md)?

questionItems.question: `claude remote-control` is run in the project directory, so it has filesystem access. Should the session be pre-loaded with any specific context (like a system prompt or CLAUDE.md)?

options.label: Just the project directory
options.description: Run `claude remote-control` in the project dir — Claude Code will pick up CLAUDE.md and project context naturally

options.label: Custom system prompt
options.description: Pass additional context like "You are helping with GSD Autopilot build" or current phase info

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on what context to provide

questions: Should the remote session be tied to the same Claude authentication the autopilot uses, or can it use whatever auth the local `claude` CLI has?

questionItems.question: Should the remote session be tied to the same Claude authentication the autopilot uses, or can it use whatever auth the local `claude` CLI has?

options.label: Use local CLI auth
options.description: Whatever `claude` is already logged in as — no special auth handling needed

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on auth approach

questions: If the user doesn't have a Max plan (required for remote-control), how should the autopilot handle it?

questionItems.question: If the user doesn't have a Max plan (required for remote-control), how should the autopilot handle it?

options.label: Warn and skip
options.description: Log a warning that remote session requires Max plan, continue autopilot without it (like tunnel graceful degradation)

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on handling auth/plan failures

questions: Should the remote session name be set to something identifiable (e.g., "GSD Autopilot - [project name]")?

questionItems.question: Should the remote session name be set to something identifiable (e.g., "GSD Autopilot - [project name]")?

options.label: Yes, branded name
options.description: Set a descriptive name so it's easy to find in the Claude session list

options.label: Default name is fine
options.description: Let `claude remote-control` use its default naming

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: More questions about session context, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about session context, or move to next area?

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Dashboard Integration

options.label: More questions
options.description: I have more to say about session context

questions: How should the remote session URL be presented in the dashboard?

questionItems.question: How should the remote session URL be presented in the dashboard?

options.label: Prominent card on Overview
options.description: A dedicated card on the Overview page with the URL, status indicator, and copy button — similar to the tunnel URL

options.label: In the header/nav bar
options.description: Always-visible link in the top navigation bar alongside other global info

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on placement

questions: Should clicking the URL open Claude Code in a new tab, or embed it in the dashboard (iframe)?

questionItems.question: Should clicking the URL open Claude Code in a new tab, or embed it in the dashboard (iframe)?

options.label: New tab (external link)
options.description: Click opens claude.ai/code in a new browser tab — simple, no iframe issues

options.label: Embedded iframe
options.description: Show the Claude Code session inside the dashboard page itself

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: Should the card show the session status (online/offline/reconnecting)?

questionItems.question: Should the card show the session status (online/offline/reconnecting)?

options.label: Yes, live status
options.description: Show green/red indicator based on whether the remote-control process is running

options.label: Just the URL
options.description: Show the URL and copy button, no real-time status

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: Should the remote session URL also appear in terminal output when autopilot starts (like the dashboard URL and tunnel URL do)?

questionItems.question: Should the remote session URL also appear in terminal output when autopilot starts (like the dashboard URL and tunnel URL do)?

options.label: Yes, show alongside others
options.description: Print the remote session URL in the startup banner next to dashboard and tunnel URLs

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion

questions: More questions about dashboard integration, or are we done?

questionItems.question: More questions about dashboard integration, or are we done?

options.label: Done, create context
options.description: That covers all four areas — create the CONTEXT.md

options.label: More questions
options.description: I have more to say about dashboard integration

questions: Which areas do you want to discuss for the devtunnel login command?

questionItems.question: Which areas do you want to discuss for the devtunnel login command?

options.label: Subcommand design
options.description: How does the user invoke this? `/gsd:autopilot login` vs separate command? What other auth-related subcommands (logout, status)?

options.label: Interactive auth flow
options.description: How to handle the browser-based login from Claude Code? What if the browser doesn't open? Timeout behavior?

options.label: Feedback and confirmation
options.description: What does the user see during/after login? Success/failure messaging? Token validation?

options.label: Auth method scope
options.description: Just devtunnel login, or also support GitHub PAT and DEVTUNNEL_TOKEN setup? One flow or multiple?

answers.Which areas do you want to discuss for t: Auth method scope, Feedback and confirmation, Interactive auth flow, Subcommand design

questions: How should the user invoke devtunnel login?

questionItems.question: How should the user invoke devtunnel login?

options.label: /gsd:autopilot login
options.description: Add 'login' as a subcommand to the existing /gsd:autopilot command (alongside status, stop)

options.label: /gsd:tunnel-login
options.description: Separate standalone slash command, not tied to /gsd:autopilot

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks whichever fits the existing command structure best

questions: Should there be companion subcommands beyond 'login'?

questionItems.question: Should there be companion subcommands beyond 'login'?

options.label: login + logout + auth-status
options.description: Full set: login to authenticate, logout to clear credentials, auth-status to check current auth state

options.label: login + auth-status only
options.description: Login and check status, but no explicit logout (users can run devtunnel user logout manually)

options.label: login only
options.description: Just the login command — keep it minimal

questions: Should /gsd:autopilot login check if already logged in before starting the flow?

questionItems.question: Should /gsd:autopilot login check if already logged in before starting the flow?

options.label: Yes, skip if logged in
options.description: Run `devtunnel user show` first — if credentials exist, show 'Already authenticated' and skip

options.label: Yes, but offer re-login
options.description: If already logged in, ask 'Already authenticated. Re-login?' before proceeding

options.label: No, always run login
options.description: Always run the login flow regardless — it'll refresh the cached token

questions: Should the login command check if devtunnel CLI is installed and offer guidance if not?

questionItems.question: Should the login command check if devtunnel CLI is installed and offer guidance if not?

options.label: Yes, with install instructions
options.description: Check for devtunnel CLI first — if missing, show platform-specific install instructions (winget, brew, etc.)

options.label: Yes, basic error only
options.description: Check for devtunnel CLI — if missing, show a simple 'devtunnel CLI not found' error with a docs link

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks the right level of guidance

answers.Should the login command check if devtun: The devtunnel.exe is in the autopilot code directory

questions: More questions about subcommand design, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about subcommand design, or move to next area?

options.label: More questions
options.description: Continue discussing subcommand design

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Interactive auth flow

questions: The devtunnel login opens a browser for auth. How should this work from Claude Code?

questionItems.question: The devtunnel login opens a browser for auth. How should this work from Claude Code?

options.label: Spawn and let OS handle
options.description: Run `devtunnel user login` which auto-opens the browser — user completes auth there, command waits for callback

options.label: Show URL for manual open
options.description: Run `devtunnel user login` but also capture and display the auth URL so the user can open it manually if the browser doesn't auto-open

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks the approach that works best in the Claude Code environment

questions: What should happen if the login process takes too long?

questionItems.question: What should happen if the login process takes too long?

options.label: 2-minute timeout
options.description: Wait up to 2 minutes for the user to complete browser auth, then show timeout error

options.label: 5-minute timeout
options.description: Wait up to 5 minutes — gives more time for slow auth flows or multi-factor

options.label: No timeout
options.description: Wait indefinitely until the devtunnel login process completes or is cancelled

questions: Should the login command support a specific auth provider choice?

questionItems.question: Should the login command support a specific auth provider choice?

options.label: Microsoft account (default)
options.description: Use `devtunnel user login` which defaults to Microsoft/Azure AD authentication

options.label: GitHub option too
options.description: Offer both: `devtunnel user login` for Microsoft and `devtunnel user login -g` for GitHub account

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks based on what devtunnel supports and what makes sense for GSD users

questions: If the devtunnel login process exits with an error (auth denied, network issue), what should happen?

questionItems.question: If the devtunnel login process exits with an error (auth denied, network issue), what should happen?

options.label: Show error + suggest retry
options.description: Display the error message and suggest running the command again

options.label: Auto-retry once
options.description: On failure, automatically retry the login once before showing error

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks appropriate error handling

questions: More questions about interactive auth flow, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about interactive auth flow, or move to next area?

options.label: More questions
options.description: Continue discussing the interactive auth flow

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Feedback and confirmation

questions: What should the user see while waiting for browser auth to complete?

questionItems.question: What should the user see while waiting for browser auth to complete?

options.label: Simple waiting message
options.description: "Waiting for browser authentication... (Press Ctrl+C to cancel)" — static text

options.label: Countdown timer
options.description: "Waiting for browser authentication... (4:32 remaining)" — shows time left

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks the right waiting indicator for the environment

questions: After successful login, what should the confirmation message include?

questionItems.question: After successful login, what should the confirmation message include?

options.label: Account name + ready
options.description: "Authenticated as user@example.com. Dev tunnels are ready for /gsd:autopilot."  — show who they logged in as

options.label: Just success
options.description: "Devtunnel login successful. Ready for /gsd:autopilot." — no account details

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks appropriate success messaging

questions: Should the command validate the token actually works after login (e.g., attempt a test API call)?

questionItems.question: Should the command validate the token actually works after login (e.g., attempt a test API call)?

options.label: Yes, validate token
options.description: After login, run `devtunnel user show` to confirm credentials are cached and valid

options.label: No, trust the exit code
options.description: If `devtunnel user login` exits 0, assume success — don't add extra verification

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks the right validation approach

questions: Should the output include a hint about next steps after login?

questionItems.question: Should the output include a hint about next steps after login?

options.label: Yes, show next command
options.description: After success: "Run /gsd:autopilot to start with remote dashboard access enabled."

options.label: No, just confirm
options.description: Just the success message — user knows what to do next

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks whether to include next steps hint

questions: More questions about feedback and confirmation, or move to next area?

questionItems.question: More questions about feedback and confirmation, or move to next area?

options.label: More questions
options.description: Continue discussing feedback and confirmation

options.label: Next area
options.description: Move on to Auth method scope

questions: Should the login command only handle `devtunnel user login`, or also offer alternative auth methods?

questionItems.question: Should the login command only handle `devtunnel user login`, or also offer alternative auth methods?

options.label: Devtunnel login only
options.description: Just wrap `devtunnel user login` — users who want GITHUB_TOKEN or DEVTUNNEL_TOKEN can set env vars manually

options.label: Login + token setup
options.description: Also offer a flow to set GITHUB_TOKEN or DEVTUNNEL_TOKEN as env vars (guided setup)

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks based on what's practical for Claude Code users

questions: When the user picks GitHub auth (`-g` flag), should the command explain the difference or just proceed?

questionItems.question: When the user picks GitHub auth (`-g` flag), should the command explain the difference or just proceed?

options.label: Ask which provider
options.description: Present a choice: "Login with Microsoft account or GitHub?" — then run the appropriate command

options.label: Default Microsoft, flag for GitHub
options.description: Default to Microsoft auth. User passes an argument like `/gsd:autopilot login github` for GitHub

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks the cleanest UX for provider selection

questions: Should the login command check if a running autopilot instance exists and warn about needing to restart?

questionItems.question: Should the login command check if a running autopilot instance exists and warn about needing to restart?

options.label: Yes, warn if running
options.description: If autopilot is running, warn that the tunnel won't pick up new credentials until restart

options.label: No, just login
options.description: Login is independent — user handles restart themselves if needed

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude picks based on likely user confusion scenarios

questions: Should the login flow detect if devtunnel.exe is missing from the autopilot directory and handle that?

questionItems.question: Should the login flow detect if devtunnel.exe is missing from the autopilot directory and handle that?

options.label: Error with clear message
options.description: If devtunnel.exe not found in expected location, show error with reinstall instructions

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude handles the missing binary case appropriately

options.label: Assume always present
options.description: The binary ships with the package — if missing, something is very wrong, basic error is fine
